column8:
I think of the Software Engineering emphasis as the one that is designed to prepare students for industry (terminal degee for professionals).

I fully support having the degree not be faculty-focused, supporting their research.  However, if the general CS degree isn't the one that prepares students for grad school, what is?  We're gonna have a hard time hiring faculty in the future if we don't retain grad-school prep as a focus.
column13:
I really love the idea of a junior core, great direction!!  I am very supportive of this idea, but I'm not yet on board with the proposed set.

In fact, I really don't like the idea of picking amongst our current courses for what should be in there.  We can pick what goes in there and it doesn't have to be complete courses.  We're not building a modular home from pre-fabricated pieces.

Something more like a CS version similar to the ACME junior core would be better.  With cohorts for the junior core.
column14: I think something needs to be articulated for why this set of courses.  I'm not sure there would be near consensus on some of these for why they belong in a junior core.
column25: I do like the breadth/depth requirements just not these and how the courses seem to have been picked.
column26: Why 4 areas, why these?  I can point to other schools where CS is split into more like 8 groups.
column31: I like these options.  To me, the key part is mentorship.  I'm not fully convinced that all of these provide that in equal or strong measure.
column32:
An internship-like experience is critical for our students, however, I'm not sure it fits (can be/should be) a part of our program.  A co-op would be a part of our program.

Also 3 credits is not enough.  6 should be a minimum, but I actually think 12+ would be ideal, if we can ensure high quality.  I suspect that 3 comes more from the scale that we feel we can provide, not what we feel students need or would benefit from.

column14:
Seems to be missing new class on agent engineering that Mike P Jones and Gordon are working on or some elements of 452 of using agents both as assistants and inside of an application. 

452 needs to be re-written to be 352 I imagine. We lean heavily on the 400-level concept for some projects and our final project. We expect students to produce capstone quality results as they should be demonstrating a culmination of skills as a CS student. There are also some things like building your own HSNW index or IVF index that Porter wanted to add that might be too advanced at a 300 level. Feels like you'd want to split it up. I think there could be 200 level, 300 level, and 400 level elements of the course currently. I think I'd not want complaints that it is too advanced for 300-level if we are just renaming it. Not sure how to achieve that.
